text:
Once upon an autumn evening, Little Red Riding Hood decided to deliver baked beans instead of usual rabbit pellets. She hopped past fields of pumpkins with their hues turning orange. A wandering owl welcomed her owl clutch eggs.

"Hopping through fields of orange," Little Doe remarked.

"Yeah!" Owlcote said. "Hop yours maybe?"

Little Red smiled. "I don't think so. Beans seem safer."

They flew higher. Under fluttering stars. Little Owlcote clucked some poetry. "You ever see ghosts?"

"Just sometimes." Little Red chuckled thinking only rabbits saw such things. "Why would I?"

"I've heard whispers."

"Oh." Little Red considered sleepy owls.

They sailed closer. A moonlit owl glided. "Would you?"

Just then rabbit hare jumped ahead. "I dare!"

"No."

"Oh yes!"

"I think not!"

They chased.

"Race you?"

"Not my thing."

They darted.

Late they soared.

"Just think." Owlcote.

She
